name: manage_tickets_tool
summary: Read and mutate the ticket queue from one tool: union of `read_tickets_tool` and `write_tickets_tool`.
Use `get` / `list` / `search` to read, and `create` / `edit` / `comment` / `transition` / `assign` to write.
constraints: One `action` per call. Eight actions in total — see the `action` parameter.
Every action that takes a `key` defaults to the agent's current ticket when `key` is omitted.
`comment` defaults `author` and `create` defaults `reporter` to the agent's name.
`list` and `search` cap at 50 tickets per call.
anti_patterns: When the agent only needs reads — register `read_tickets_tool` instead. Smaller surface = fewer mistakes.
When the agent should never read tickets it does not own — register `write_tickets_tool` to block listing/searching.
To find code or files in the working directory — use `grep_tool` / `glob_tool`.
cautions: IMPORTANT: status transitions follow a state machine. Allowed: `Todo↔InProgress`, `InProgress↔InReview`, `InProgress`/`InReview` → `Done`/`Failed`. Other moves return an error.
IMPORTANT: `Done` and `Failed` are terminal. Use `InReview` if a reviewer should be able to send the ticket back.
IMPORTANT: transitioning to `Todo` clears the assignee.
ALWAYS settle your current ticket before the response ends. Returning text without `transition` leaves the ticket `InProgress` and the loop re-picks it.
output: Reads (`get`): markdown ticket block with key, status, type, reporter, assignee, description, comments.
Reads (`list` / `search`): bullet lines `- KEY [STATUS] [TYPE] @assignee — summary`. `(no matching tickets)` when empty.
Writes: plain text confirmation, or an error describing the failed action.

key.description: Ticket key (e.g. `TICKET-3`). Used by `get`, `edit`, `comment`, `transition`, `assign`. Defaults to the agent's current ticket. Ignored by `create`, `list`, `search`.

status.description: For `list`: filter by status. For `transition`: target status. One of `Todo`, `InProgress`, `InReview`, `Done`, `Failed`.

assignee.description: For `list`: filter by assignee (excludes unassigned tickets). For `assign`: agent or human name to take ownership; empty string clears it.

query.description: For `search`: case-insensitive substring matched against summary and description.

type.description: For `create`: ticket type (e.g. `task`, `bug`). Defaults to `task`. Determines which agents can pick it up via Path B.

reporter.description: For `create`: who filed the ticket. Defaults to the agent's name.

author.description: For `comment`: who wrote the comment. Defaults to the agent's name.
